_patch_meta.description: VCB-013 halt-resolution: H2896A tov mti=884 — first-time classification of all 141 previously-pending verses (75 set-aside: 59 physical_only descriptors of objects/persons/land/aesthetics + 16 no_inner_being including 'good or bad' merism + 'feast/holiday' idioms + good-old-age formula; 66 relevant: 14 placed in existing groups 884-002/003/004/005/006, 52 placed in 3 new groups proposed). 3 new groups: 884-007 (Gen 1 creation evaluative pronouncement, 7 verses, anchor Gen 1:31), 884-008 (volitional-preference idiom 'as it pleases', 40 verses, anchor Jer 26:14), 884-009 (inner well-being state, 5 verses, anchor Est 5:9). DF-HR-002 documents the new-group decision. MIXED routing per VC §6.3 Step 3.

record.context_description: Term names God's evaluative pronouncement on his creation — the divine inner-being action of judging-and-declaring-good in Gen 1, where the Creator inspects what he has made and pronounces it good or very good

Term names what is good-in-the-eyes-of / pleasing to / preferred-by an actor — the volitional-preference idiom in which the term is the predicate of someone's will or evaluative agreement, naming what they choose, prefer, agree to, or judge fitting
Term names a state of inner well-being — the shalom-condition of being-well, prospering, glad-of-heart, or well-disposed — the inner-being state experienced or sought by the human person

record.notes: Divine evaluative pronouncement on created light — God's inner-being action of judging-as-good
Divine evaluative pronouncement on dry land/seas
Divine evaluative pronouncement on vegetation

Divine evaluative pronouncement on land animals
Divine evaluative pronouncement on the whole creation — "very good"
"Good for food" — physical quality of trees in Eden
"Gold of that land is good" — physical-quality descriptor of gold
"Daughters of man were attractive" (tovat) — physical-aesthetic descriptor
"Good old age" — names completed life-quality at death; tov modifies physical-temporal age

"Write as you please" — king to Esther/Mordecai

"Beautiful houses desolate" — physical-aesthetic of buildings

"Saying of the soldering, It is good" — craftsman's evaluative judgement on workmanship; inner-being assessment

"If it seems good to you to come" / "go wherever you think good and right" — Nebuzaradan to Jeremiah; volitional preference

"We had plenty of food and prospered" — Jeremiah's opponents recalling their false prosperity in idolatry; fits 884-006 (prophetic verdict on conduct as not-good — here ironic, the people's self-justification is itself the prophetic indictment) — borderline; could also be 884-NEW-WB. Lean: 884-006 since this is reported speech in a prophetic indictment context.
"Pure gold (tov) is changed" — physical-quality of gold (figurative for Zion's former glory; tov names the physical metal in the vehicle)
"Good soil" — figurative-vine; tov names physical agricultural quality in the vehicle
"Good pieces of meat" — Ezekiel's pot allegory; tov names physical-quality of meat in vehicle
"Choice and best of Lebanon" (trees) — figurative; tov of physical trees
"Good pasture, good grazing land" — Ezekiel's shepherd allegory; the relational tenor (God shepherding faithfully) is in the broader allegory; tov here qualifies physical pasture in the vehicle
"Better in appearance, fatter in flesh" — physical-aesthetic comparison

"Their shade is good" (trees of high places used in idolatry) — physical-quality of shade; the verse's inner-being weight (idolatry indictment) is in the surrounding sacrificial-action verbs, not in tov
"Rich (good) treasures" — physical wealth taken into pagan temples
"Are you better than these kingdoms?" — Amos' rhetorical comparative; warning to Israel; fits 884-004 (comparative wisdom good as judgement-frame)

"Graceful (tov) and of deadly charms" (prostitute Nineveh) — figurative-physical aesthetic of personification; the inner-being weight is in the figurative-political indictment, not in tov which qualifies the prostitute-figure's aesthetic
"If it seems good to you, give me my wages" — symbolic shepherd to flock; volitional preference
